data.title: Chart Industries tapped as Top Pick by Morgan Stanley after Flowserve merger
data.description:
Chart Industries (NYSE:GTLS) was named as a Top Pick by Morgan Stanley analysts Friday following its $19B all-stock merger with Flowserve (FLS), which the bank said should further boost what already was a compelling growth and relative valuation story for the shares.

The deal pulls accelerates Chart's (NYSE:GTLS) initiatives such as A/M expansion, free cash flow durability, and delevering, but regardless, Morgan Stanley analyst Daniel Kutz said he views the company's organic growth prospects as "compelling, and deal-related upside as a potential incremental valuation boost."

Deal synergy targets are compelling if realized, particularly the guided commercial synergy target of a 200 bps top line boost to compound annual growth rate, according to Kutz, but even as some question whether such a target is achievable, Chart's (GTLS) organic growth prospects already are compelling; if the company come close to the growth targets, the shares are "meaningfully" undervalued, the analyst said.

In light of the merger, and including confirmation that the new company would retain Flowserve's (FLS) dividend - already at ~30% of Flowserve's standalone free cash flow -  plus the stronger liquidity position of the pro forma entity, Kutz now assumes the merged company's total shareholder return percentage FCF "would be ~50%+ of FCF immediately post transaction close, comprised of both dividends and share buybacks."

data.title: Lilly, Novo, and Amgen set for key obesity trial readouts at upcoming ADA event
data.description:
Leading weight loss drug developers Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY), Novo Nordisk (NVO), and Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) are scheduled to present major clinical trial readouts at the American Diabetes Association's (ADA) 85th Scientific Sessions taking place June 20-23 in Chicago.

Lilly (NYSE:LLY) is set to post results for its investigational oral weight loss therapy, orforglipron, from its ACHIEVE-1 Phase 3 trial for adults with type 2 diabetes at an ADA-sponsored symposium on June 21.

The Indiana-based drugmaker released topline data from ACHIEVE-1 in April, noting that the global trial, the first of seven Phase 3 studies for orforglipron, reached the main goal as the once-daily pill cut patients’ weight by ~8% on average over 40 weeks.

Novo Nordisk (NVO) is also set for a symposium presentation to release data from its REDEFINE 1 and REDEFINE 2 Phase 3 trials for its next-gen weight loss therapy, CagriSema on June 22.

Shares of the Danish drugmaker plunged in December after the once-weekly injectable fell short of the company’s initial expectations in its REDEFINE 1 Phase 3 trial, which enrolled obese or overweight adults with one or more associated illnesses.

“The presentation of the CagriSema REDEFINE 1 and 2 trials are the first ever Phase 3 data presented on a GLP-1 and amylin receptor agonist combination, offering insights into the potential of this investigational medicine,” the company said ahead of the event.

Meanwhile, the safety of Amgen’s (NASDAQ:AMGN) experimental weight loss therapy MariTide will be in focus when the Thousand Oaks, California-based firm shares full data from a Phase 2 study for the injectable on June 23.

Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) shares fell in November after the company reported that MariTide caused an average weight loss of up to 20% over 52 weeks. in the trial, which enrolled individuals with obesity or overweight conditions but without Type 2 diabetes.

Previously, a report from Cantor Fitzgerald indicated that a potential loss of bone mineral density could be a safety risk linked to MariTide, which targets gut hormone receptors (GLP-1) as well as glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide receptors.

Other notable weight loss drug developers selected for ADA presentations include Altimmune (ALT), Metsera (MTSR), and Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX).

data.title: Apple shareholders file lawsuit over AI disclosures - report
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) was sued by shareholders in a proposed class action, accusing the tech giant of downplaying the time needed to integrate advanced AI-based features into its Siri assistant, reported Reuters on Friday.

According to the report, the complaint, which was filed in San Francisco federal court, alleges that this misrepresentation hurt iPhone sales and negatively impacted Apple’s stock price.

The complaint also seeks unspecified damages for shareholders who lost money in the year ending June 9.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Powell goes to DC, FedEx earnings, and big banks get a stress test
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - June 23

  • Volatility watch - The conflict between Israel and Iran will continue to be front and center for equity and commodities markets. Nektar Therapeutics (NKTR) and INmune Bio (INMB) have a higher level of implied volatility than normal based on options trading. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Columbus Circle Capital Corp. I (CCCMU), Coinsilium Group (OTCQB:CINGF), and Regencell (RGC). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include PG&E (PCG) and Copart (CPRT).
  • IPO watch - Jefferson Capital (JCAP) is expected to price its IPO and begin to trade. The consumer finance company is offering 625,000 shares and selling shareholders are offering 9.4 million shares. At the top end of the expected pricing range of $15.00 to $17.00, the company would be valued at ~$1.10 billion.
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Factset Research (FDS) and KB Home (KBH).
  • Dividend watch - Some companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Disney (DIS), Humana (HUM), Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP), and Philip Morris (PM).
  • All day - Flash global June PMI reports will give traders a broad view of economic activity and business sentiment for the month.
  • All day - The four-day Samsara Beyond event will begin. The event will highlight the company's innovations in safety, efficiency, and sustainability for connected operations. Samsara (IOT) will also hold an Investor Day event in connection with Samsara Beyond.
  • 10:00 a.m. The Existing Home Sales report will be released by the National Association of Realtors.

 

Tuesday - June 24

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include FedEx (FDX) and Carnival (CCL). Options trading implies a double-digit share price move for BlackBerry (BB) after it reports.
  • All day - HPE (HPE) CEO Antonio Neri will deliver a keynote address at the HPE Discover event in Las Vegas. The four-day event will have a strong focus on networking, AI, and hybrid cloud, with announcements expected on HPE GreenLake, next-gen storage, and AI-native networks.
  • All day - Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell to testify to the House Financial Services Committee.
  • All day - Traders will be watching the two-day NATO Summit at The Hague. Notably, the STOXX Europe Aerospace & Defense ETF (EUAD) is up 66% this year as Europe has looked to strengthen itself.
  • All day - CF Industries (CF) will hold an Investor Day event.
  • All day - Some of the notable companies due to appear at the Northland Growth Conference include FatPipe (FATN), Asure Software (ASUR), Innventure (INV), and TeraWulf (WULF).
  • 9:00 a.m. Economists have the release of the S&P/Case-Shiller House Price Index circled as a key update for the housing market
  • 10:15 a.m. Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack will participate in a panel before the Barclays-CEPR Monetary Policy Forum 2025.
  • 12:30 p.m. New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams gives keynote remarks to the Center for Economic Growth and NY CREATES.

 

Wednesday - June 25

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Micron Technology (MU), Paychex (PAYX), General Mills (GIS), and Jefferies (JEF). Options trading implies a double-digit share price move for Winnebago (WGO) after it reports.
  • All day - Equinix (EQIX) will host an Analyst Day event. The company's leadership team will present and participate in Q&A.
  • All day - Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell will testify to the Senate Banking Committee.
  • 10:00 a.m. Acadia Pharmaceuticals (ACAD) will host an R&D day in New York City to discuss its neurological and rare diseases pipeline. The stock has rallied after similar events in the past.
  • 10:00 a.m. The New Home Sales report will be released by the U.S. Census Bureau.
  • 2:00 p.m. Nvidia (NVDA) will hold its annual meeting of shareholders. Proposals up for vote include director appointments, and shareholder proposals on the rules for special meetings and workforce data reporting.

 

Thursday - June 26

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Nike (NKE), McCormick (MKC), Walgreens Boots Alliance (WBA), and Lindsay (LNN). Options trading implies a double-digit share price move for American Outdoor Brands (AOUT) after it reports.
  • All day - Genpact (G) will hold an Investor Day event. The focus will be on Genpact’s AI-driven transformation strategy, data and domain-driven solutions, and future growth roadmap.
  • All day - It will be another active day of Fedspeak, with Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin, Federal Reserve Board Governor Michael Barr, and Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack all scheduled to give talks.
  • 9:00 a.m. Crinetics Pharmaceuticals (CRNX) will host an in-person and virtual R&D Day in New York. The event will provide an update including data on the company's early-stage pipeline assets, next steps, and portfolio strategy to drive long-term value.

 

Friday - June 27

  • 8:30 a.m. The core PCE price index report for May will be released. The Fed's favored inflation gauge was up 2.5% year-over-year in April.
  • 4:30 p.m. The Federal Reserve will release the results of its annual bank stress tests. The release of the stress test results has been a major event for investors in the past, particularly those with exposure to large U.S. banks and the broader financial sector.
  • Postmarket - The annual Russell Index reconstitution becomes effective. Investors should prepare for increased volatility and potential price dislocations in stocks being added to or removed from the Russell indexes.

data.title: Richmond Fed's Barkin says tariff risks leave no rush to cut rates - report
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of Richmond President Thomas Barkin said Friday that there is no urgency to reduce interest rates, pointing to risks that tariffs could reheat inflation while the labor market and consumer spending remain resilient.

"I don't think the data gives us any rush to cut," Barkin said in an interview with Reuters, noting "I am very conscious that we've not been at our inflation target for four years."

Businesses in his district still see price gains coming later in the year as new tariffs kick in, he said.

Still, "I'm comfortable with where we are...Core inflation is still over target. Being modestly restrictive is a good way to address that."

On the Fed's lastest rate projections, Barkin took note of the close split of opinion, with seven officials penciling in no cuts (up from four in the March projections), and eight calling for two cuts (down from 9). Overall, the Fed still sees two quarter-point rate cuts this year, based on the median projection.

But Barkin expects tariffs to potentially be passed down fully to consumers over the coming months, and, consequently, lift prices. "I don't have a lot of conviction about where (trade) policy is going to be. I just have to accept that" possibility, he told Reuters. 

"There will be some inflationary impact. It's hard to know how much," he added. 
 
Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: GlobalData.TSLombard's Dario Perkins lays out three cases for current market sentiment
data.description:
Wall Street has rebounded spectacularly from its April lows, partly helped by positive trade developments. U.S. President Donald Trump's "Libration Day" announcement sent the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) briefly spiraling into bear market territory in early April, but a whopping +23.7% advance since has brought the gauge back near record levels.

"So here's the debate we are having in global macro right now - sentiment has improved since Liberation Day but the impact of tariffs is yet to show up. Hard data are likely to deteriorate while soft data improve (worse case scenarios avoided). There are three competing views," Dario Perkins, managing director of global macro at GlobalData.TSLombard, said on X (formerly Twitter) on Friday. 

One of the chief reasons for Wall Street's recovery has been Trump's dialing back of aggressive trade barriers and rhetoric to give other countries a chance to negotiate with the U.S. A thawing in relations with China has also helped.

Market participants have labeled Trump's pattern of issuing reversals on trade policy as "Trump Always Chickens Out" (TACO) - an acronym that was first coined by a Financial Times columnist in May.

"BULL CASE - TACO. Stocks will look through any deterioration in the hard data because they capture Liberation Day policy conditions. As things settle down, pent-up demand will kick in & economy will rebound. Don't forget tariffs are one-time hit to prices (& therefore real GDP)," Perkins said. 

"BEAR CASE - Damage has been done. Current polices are at worse end of pre-Liberation Day expectations. Corporate profits will decline, which will eventually trigger job losses and recession reflexivity. Monetary policy remains too tight and the (Federal Reserve) is now falling behind the curve," he added.

The Fed on Wednesday kept its key policy rate steady and stressed that it was well positioned to stick to a wait-and-see approach while keeping an eye out for the full effect of tariffs to show up in economic data.

"MUDDLE THROUGH - market guardrails mean we always avoid worse case scenarios but market calm emboldens the administration to keep doing crazy s***. Trump put but Trump call. Uncertainty lingers and current policies damage the economy, but not to the extent they trigger recession," Perkins said. 

The GlobalData.TSLombard economist said he had been in the "muddle through" camp all year.

"By the way, the bull case for the rest of the world is that the U.S. muddles through," he added.

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Tech Voices: Tesla's China power deal, Coinbase in EU, Meta AI glasses
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's daily roundup of remarks and statements that could impact the technology sector or individual stocks.

  • Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) has signed a deal to build China's largest grid-scale energy storage plant.

"The grid-side energy storage power station is a 'smart regulator' for urban electricity, which can flexibly adjust grid resources, effectively solve the pressure of urban power supply and ensure the safe, stable and efficient electricity demand of the city. After completion, this project is expected to become the largest grid-side energy storage project in China," Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) said in a translated post on Weibo. 

  • Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) has received a Markets in Crypto Assets, or MiCA, license from Luxembourg regulators that will allow it to offer its full suite of crypto products to all 27 members of the European Union. 

"Over the past few years, Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) has worked closely with regulators across Europe, securing licenses in Germany, France, Ireland, Italy, The Netherlands and Spain. Now, with MiCA, we’re uniting these efforts under a single framework, enabling millions of Europeans to access regulated, trusted, and secure crypto services," Coinbase (COIN) said in a blog post.

  • Meta (NASDAQ:META) and EssilorLuxottica (OTCPK:ESLOY) (OTCPK:ESLOF) have launched a line of Oakley-branded AI glasses for athletes and sports fans.

"Oakley Meta glasses are a new product line that will combine Oakley’s signature design DNA with Meta’s industry-leading technology to give you deeper insights into your physical capabilities and help you share your biggest wins – on and off the field," Meta (NASDAQ:META) said in a blog post.

"Glasses have emerged as a major new hardware category of the AI era," Meta (META) added.

Prices for the glasses, called HSTN, will start at $399. The companies already market smart glasses under the Ray-Ban brand.

data.title: Kroger outlines closure of 60 stores and raises identical sales guidance to 2.25%-3.25% amid e-commerce momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: The Kroger Co. (KR) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Ronald L. Sargent, Chairman of the Board & Interim CEO, stated that after nearly four months as CEO, his top priorities are to position Kroger for long-term growth, accelerate top line sales, and "run great stores." He emphasized a renewed focus on the core business, a growth culture, and capital allocation toward high-return projects, including accelerated new store openings. Sargent announced, "We are making meaningful changes to the business to create a culture that benefits our customers and our associates, while improving long-term shareholder value."
  • Sargent shared that the company is reviewing noncore assets, restructuring leadership, and consolidating divisions. Notably, Kroger created a new e-commerce business unit under Chief Digital Officer Yael Cosset and appointed Joe Kelley as Senior Vice President of Retail Divisions. Sargent stated, "We are on track to complete 30 major storing projects in 2025."
  • Sargent revealed plans to close approximately 60 stores over the next 18 months, explaining, "We don't take these decisions lightly, but this will make the company more efficient."
  • Sargent highlighted that Kroger is lowering prices on more than 2,000 additional products and that "Our Brands grew faster than national brands for the seventh consecutive quarter."
  • David John Christopher Kennerley, Chief Financial Officer, commented, "First, capital allocation. We'll be highly disciplined in how we deploy capital, ensuring we invest in projects that generate strong returns with clear objective of improving ROIC over time."
  • Kennerley added, "My focus will be on ensuring we prioritize our resources to drive profitable market share growth, including an acceleration of storing projects and more competitive pricing."

Outlook

  • Kennerley provided updated guidance: "We are raising our identical sales without fuel guidance to a new range of 2.25% to 3.25%. We expect second quarter identical sales without fuel to be roughly at the midpoint of our full year guidance range." He reaffirmed full year guidance for net operating profit and adjusted earnings per share, noting the macroeconomic environment remains uncertain.
  • The company expects store closures to have "a modest financial benefit," with efficiencies reinvested into the customer experience, not impacting full year guidance.

Financial Results

  • Kroger reported identical sales without fuel growth of 3.2% for Q1 2025, led by "strong pharmacy, e-commerce and fresh sales," according to Kennerley.
  • Adjusted net earnings per diluted share were $1.49 in Q1, representing a 4% increase.
  • The FIFO gross margin rate, excluding rent, depreciation, amortization, fuel and adjustment items, increased 79 basis points compared to the same period last year. Excluding the effect from the sale of Kroger Specialty Pharmacy, the FIFO gross margin rate improved by 33 basis points.
  • Operating, general and administrative rate, excluding fuel and adjustment items, increased 63 basis points, primarily due to the sale of Kroger Specialty Pharmacy and a pension plan contribution.
  • Adjusted FIFO operating profit was $1.5 billion for the quarter.
  • E-commerce sales grew 15% in the first quarter, with Kennerley noting, "We increased our rate of profit improvement from our previous record improvement in the fourth quarter of 2024."
  • Fuel sales and profitability were lower than last year, and are expected to remain a headwind for the year.
  • Net total debt to adjusted EBITDA was 1.69 at quarter end.
  • The $5 billion ASR program is expected to be completed by no later than the third fiscal quarter of 2025, with open market share repurchases under the remaining $2.5 billion authorization to follow.

Q&A

  • Edward Joseph Kelly, Wells Fargo: Asked about pricing strategy and value perception. Sargent responded, "We do intend to continue to invest in lower prices...these pricing investments resulted in better sales, better gross margin and happier customers."
  • Kelly followed up on e-commerce profitability. Sargent replied, "We are seeing improvements in profitability at an increasing rate. But to be clear, on the profitability, we're not profitable at this point."
  • John Edward Heinbockel, Guggenheim: Inquired about noncore asset strategy and capital allocation. Sargent defined core as "things that exists in our company that are dedicated to serving our customers," while Kennerley noted, "Remodels sit somewhere in the middle of our average return rate."
  • Robert Frederick Ohmes, BofA: Queried about sales tailwinds and inflation. Kennerley stated, "We saw inflation just under 2% for the quarter. We guided to 1.5% to 2.5% for the year."
  • Simeon Ari Gutman, Morgan Stanley: Asked about market share. Sargent said, "We did see significant improvements in Q1 and we saw market share gains in markets where we have added stores."
  • Paul Lawrence Lejuez, Citigroup: Asked about Our Brands and regional differences. Sargent said, "We had another strong quarter in Our Brands...I believe there's a big opportunity for Our Brands products that could accelerate this even further in the years ahead."
  • Michael Lasser, UBS: Addressed the possibility of e-commerce cannibalizing center store. Sargent responded, "We're seeing improved grocery center store trends."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were generally constructive but pressed for more detail on e-commerce profitability, margin offsets, and the impact of store closures, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone focused on risk mitigation and execution.
  • Management maintained a confident and proactive tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, with Sargent stating, "We are confident that by staying true to these priorities, we will generate long-term growth and attractive shareholder returns."
  • Compared to the prior quarter, management’s tone is more action-oriented and forthcoming about strategic shifts, especially regarding store closures and leadership changes.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company raised identical sales guidance this quarter to 2.25%-3.25%, compared to the prior quarter's guidance of 2%-3%.
  • Strategic focus shifted from simply accelerating new store openings to also closing approximately 60 underperforming stores and reviewing noncore assets.
  • Leadership restructuring was more pronounced, with additional appointments and consolidation of divisions.
  • E-commerce growth accelerated from 10% in Q4 2024 to 15% in Q1 2025.
  • Analysts continued to focus on profitability, pricing, and digital transformation, while management adopted a more proactive, growth-oriented stance.

Risks and Concerns

  • Sargent acknowledged the "uncertain economic environment" affecting customer spending and the need to continue investing in value and promotions.
  • The company cited fuel as a continuing headwind for the year.
  • Management is monitoring potential tariff impacts, but currently expects "a smaller business impact than some of our competitors."
  • Labor relations remain in focus, with ongoing negotiations and a recent 14-day strike at King Soopers stores in Colorado.
  • Store closures present potential operational risks but are intended to drive efficiency.

Final Takeaway

Kroger’s management emphasized that the company is accelerating its transformation by focusing on core business growth, driving e-commerce momentum, and optimizing its store network, including the closure of approximately 60 underperforming stores over the next 18 months. The company raised its identical sales guidance, reaffirmed its commitment to disciplined capital allocation, and outlined plans to reinvest efficiencies into the customer experience. Leadership restructuring and strategic investments in digital and fresh offerings position Kroger for long-term growth despite continued macroeconomic uncertainty and headwinds in fuel and labor.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Capricor falls after report on AdCom meeting cancellation (update)
data.description:
Capricor Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CAPR) plunged 32% following a trading halt on Friday after Stat News reported that the new head of the FDA unit responsible for biologic drugs has canceled an advisory committee meeting scheduled to review the company’s lead asset, Deramiocel.

The report followed the abrupt departure of Nicole Verdun, director of the cell and gene therapy division of the FDA’s Center for Biologics Research and Evaluation (CBER) on Wednesday, potentially over disagreements with CBER head Dr. Vinay Prasad.

According to people familiar with the matter, an advisory committee meeting scheduled by Verdun to discuss Deramiocel, currently under the FDA’s priority review for Duchenne muscular dystrophy, was unilaterally canceled by Dr. Prasad, who was uncertain about the drug’s efficacy and safety.

Earlier in the day, CAPR posted four-year data from an open-label extension trial for Deramiocel, noting that DMD patients in its HOPE-2 PLE study witnessed a median change of -0.5 points compared to baseline over four years in an unspecified metric.

Additionally, the performance of the upper limb indicated an average decline of 0.6 points in the fourth year among deramiocel-treated patients compared to 1.8 points in the first year, the company said, implying that its experimental drug can slow skeletal muscle disease progression.

The cell therapy continued to show a favorable tolerability profile over the entire duration of the study.

However, Oppenheimer analyst Leland Gershell noted that their departures shouldn’t affect the ongoing review of Deramiocel at the CBER, which recently scheduled an AdCom meeting on July 30 to discuss the company’s marketing application.

“The company continues to prepare for the anticipated advisory committee meeting, for which timing is presumably July 31, pending confirmation or change,” Bloomberg News reported, quoting Gershell. Capricor (NASDAQ:CAPR) was not immediately available for Seeking Alpha’s requests for comments.

Update 2.29 PM EST: Adds latest share price move and details from Stat News report

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: T, CAT, MRVL, HOOD
data.description:
In the latest round of notable actions from Seeking Alpha analysts, Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT) has been downgraded due to margin pressures, while Marvell (NASDAQ:MRVL) has received a positive outlook driven by its AI segment growth potential. Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD) faces valuation concerns despite strong operational metrics, and AT&T (NYSE:T) shows resilience amid macroeconomic uncertainties.

Upgrades

  • Marvell Technology, Inc. (NASDAQ:MRVL): Upgrade Sell to Buy by Tech Stock Pros. The upgrade follows the company’s Custom AI Investor Event which showcased strong long-term growth prospects in the AI sector, with data center TAM expected to reach $94B by 2028.

    “We were wrong on the short term… This was not because the fundamental analysis was wrong, but because we didn’t listen to the stock price performance YTD, which, at the time, was down roughly 42%. The near-term negatives of this bearish thesis were already priced into the stock, particularly with the stock selling off post-earnings two quarters in a row.”

  • AT&T Inc. (NYSE:T): Upgrade Neutral to Buy by Livy Investment Research. The analysts see AT&T well-positioned to weather economic headwinds with proactive customer initiatives, strong free cash flow, and an upcoming share repurchase program.

    “AT&T enters this cycle from a position of relative strength. The company has proactively prepared for demand normalization and macro uncertainty through disciplined cost management, and customer-focused strategies… This strength also translates into durable FCF, which is driving steady progress towards AT&T’s 2.5x deleveraging target and funding its newly launched $10 billion share repurchase program.”

Downgrades

  • Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE:CAT): Downgrade to Neutral by Vladimir Dimitrov, CFA. The downgrade reflects ongoing margin deterioration, with operating and net profits falling by nearly 25% year-over-year despite the stock trading near record-high multiples.

    “While sales fell by 10%, operating and net profits collapsed by nearly a quarter from a year ago… The guidance for the current quarter was for even lower profit margins, even before considering the major cost headwinds from the recent tariffs… CAT’s current premium over its book value of equity is already way too high when considering the company’s operating margin on a trailing twelve months basis.”

  • Robinhood Markets, Inc. (NASDAQ:HOOD): Downgrade Buy to Neutral by Julian Lin. Despite impressive growth in transaction revenues and platform assets, the analyst believes the current valuation is too stretched, pricing in many years of growth.

    “The current valuation has gotten ahead of itself, as it is pricing in many years of growth. I am downgrading the stock to a neutral rating until the fundamentals catch up with the stock price… It might seem curious to be downgrading the stock even with strong revenue growth, torrid margin expansion, a net cash balance sheet, and ongoing share repurchase program, but the valuation looks quite stretched to me.”
